item.text: Batch 2 is scoped to the ruler only. I’m checking the existing timeline editor and playback hooks first so the new `TimeRuler` matches current time formatting and can drop into the later canvas work without rework.

Context is clear now: the existing formatter already lives in `coordinate-utils`, and the library’s ruler math is `pixelsPerSecond = scaleWidth / scale` with a reserved `startLeft` gutter. I’m implementing a standalone `TimeRuler` against those rules so T3 can compose it directly.

item.text: I have the API shape and interaction model pinned down. I’m adding `TimeRuler.tsx` as a standalone controlled component now, with tick virtualization against its visible width and a pointer threshold so click and scrub stay distinct.
The new ruler compiles. I’m doing one small hardening pass before checkpointing: guarding the resize observer so this component won’t blow up in tests or any non-browser render path.

item.text: Batch 3 is the canvas shell, so I’m defining it to solve three things together: content width from scale math, horizontal scroll sync with `TimeRuler`, and an imperative cursor path that updates without React state. After that I’ll wire the track-label scroll sync into the component API so T6 can swap it in cleanly.

The current `TimelineEditor` still gives me the future prop contract: fixed row height, computed scale count, shared `timelineRef`, and sibling label pane. I’m coding `TimelineCanvas` around that exact shape so the later swap is mostly JSX replacement rather than another structural rewrite.

The shell compiles. I’m tightening one layout detail before checkpointing: the content layer should simply fill the scroll viewport, not subtract the ruler height again, because the ruler already sits outside the scroll pane.

item.text: Batch 4 touches the two regression-prone seams: live resize preview and DOM lookup math. I’m reading the resize hook and drop-position helpers first so the new canvas callbacks stay signature-compatible and the selector migration doesn’t break the still-mounted library path before T6.

I’ve got the two transition constraints clear now. Resize needs to be fully local to `TimelineCanvas` during pointer movement, while the DOM lookup migration has to support both the old library DOM and the new canvas DOM until T6 actually swaps the component in.

The resize path compiles. I’m verifying two things by inspection before checkpointing: that the live geometry now comes from an override map during drag, and that the selector migration prefers the new canvas DOM while still falling back to the old library DOM until T6.

One correctness issue showed up in inspection: the resize-end callback should not depend on React state having flushed the last pointermove. I’m fixing that by keeping the latest transient geometry in a ref alongside state so the final callback always gets the exact last drag position.

TypeScript verification passes.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx","src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts","src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx",".megaplan/plans/replace-xzdarcy-react-20260325-2054/execution_batch_4.json"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,240p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts","sed -n '1,340p' src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx","sed -n '1,260p' src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts","sed -n '1,240p' src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx","sed -n '1,260p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts","sed -n '1,360p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts","rg -n \"timeline-editor-edit-area|ReactVirtualized__Grid|timeline-canvas-edit-area|clientXToTime|resizeOverrides|onActionResizeStart|onActionResizing|onActionResizeEnd|setPointerCapture|clearResizeOverride\" src/tools/video-editor","npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false"],"deviations":[],"task_updates":[{"task_id":"T4","status":"done","executor_notes":"Updated `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` with left/right 8px pointer resize handles on each action wrapper. The component now owns transient resize geometry in `resizeOverrides`, mirrored in `resizePreviewRef` so pointerup uses the latest drag position without waiting for React state. Pointerdown captures the handle pointer, fires `onActionResizeStart({ action, row, dir })`, pointermove updates the transient geometry and fires `onActionResizing({ action, row, start, end, dir })`, and pointerup fires `onActionResizeEnd({ action, row, start, end, dir })` before clearing the override. This preserves `useClipResize.ts` callback compatibility without any hook changes. item.text: Batch 5 is the actual component swap. I’m checking the remaining `onChange` usage and the track-label pane behavior first so the editor moves to `TimelineCanvas` without leaving a dead resize path or a scroll-alignment regression behind.

import { useMemo } from 'react';
import { useProjectSelectionContext } from '@/shared/contexts/ProjectContext';
import { useVideoEditorRuntime } from '@/tools/video-editor/contexts/DataProviderContext';
import { ROW_HEIGHT, TIMELINE_START_LEFT } from '@/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils';
import { useAssetManagement } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement';
import { useClipEditing } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing';
import { useClipResize } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize';
import { useTimelineData } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData';
import { useDragCoordinator } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator';
import { useExternalDrop } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop';
import { useTimelinePlayback } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePlayback';
import type {
  TimelineChromeContextValue,
  TimelineEditorContextValue,
  TimelinePlaybackContextValue,
  UseTimelineStateResult,
} from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types';
import { useTimelineTrackManagement } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement';

export type { SaveStatus, RenderStatus, EditorPreferences } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData';

export function useTimelineState(): UseTimelineStateResult {
  const runtime = useVideoEditorRuntime();
  const playback = useTimelinePlayback();
  const dataHook = useTimelineData();
  const { selectedProjectId } = useProjectSelectionContext();

  const dragCoordinator = useDragCoordinator({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    scale: dataHook.scale,
    scaleWidth: dataHook.scaleWidth,
    startLeft: TIMELINE_START_LEFT,
    rowHeight: ROW_HEIGHT,
  });

  const assetManagement = useAssetManagement({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    selectedTrackId: dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    selectedProjectId,
    setSelectedClipId: dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    patchRegistry: dataHook.patchRegistry,
    registerAsset: dataHook.registerAsset,
    uploadAsset: dataHook.uploadAsset,
    invalidateAssetRegistry: dataHook.invalidateAssetRegistry,
    resolveAssetUrl: runtime.provider.resolveAssetUrl.bind(runtime.provider),
  });

  const externalDrop = useExternalDrop({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    scale: dataHook.scale,
    scaleWidth: dataHook.scaleWidth,
    selectedTrackId: dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    patchRegistry: dataHook.patchRegistry,
    registerAsset: dataHook.registerAsset,
    uploadAsset: dataHook.uploadAsset,
    invalidateAssetRegistry: dataHook.invalidateAssetRegistry,
    resolveAssetUrl: runtime.provider.resolveAssetUrl.bind(runtime.provider),
    coordinator: dragCoordinator.coordinator,
    registerGenerationAsset: assetManagement.registerGenerationAsset,
    uploadImageGeneration: assetManagement.uploadImageGeneration,
    handleAssetDrop: assetManagement.handleAssetDrop,
  });

  const clipResize = useClipResize({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
  });

  const clipEditing = useClipEditing({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    resolvedConfig: dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    selectedClipId: dataHook.selectedClipId,
    selectedTrack: dataHook.selectedTrack,
    currentTime: playback.currentTime,
    setSelectedClipId: dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    applyResolvedConfigEdit: dataHook.applyResolvedConfigEdit,
  });

  const trackManagement = useTimelineTrackManagement({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    resolvedConfig: dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    selectedClipId: dataHook.selectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    applyResolvedConfigEdit: dataHook.applyResolvedConfigEdit,
  });

  const editor = useMemo<TimelineEditorContextValue>(() => ({
    data: dataHook.data,
    resolvedConfig: dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    selectedClipId: dataHook.selectedClipId,
    selectedTrackId: dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    selectedClip: dataHook.selectedClip,
    selectedTrack: dataHook.selectedTrack,
    selectedClipHasPredecessor: dataHook.selectedClipHasPredecessor,
    compositionSize: dataHook.compositionSize,
    trackScaleMap: dataHook.trackScaleMap,
    scale: dataHook.scale,
    scaleWidth: dataHook.scaleWidth,
    isLoading: dataHook.isLoading,
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    coordinator: dragCoordinator.coordinator,
    indicatorRef: dragCoordinator.indicatorRef,
    editAreaRef: dragCoordinator.editAreaRef,
    preferences: dataHook.preferences,
    timelineRef: playback.timelineRef,
    timelineWrapperRef: playback.timelineWrapperRef,
    setSelectedClipId: dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    setActiveClipTab: dataHook.setActiveClipTab,
    setAssetPanelState: dataHook.setAssetPanelState,
    registerGenerationAsset: assetManagement.registerGenerationAsset,
    onCursorDrag: playback.onCursorDrag,
    onClickTimeArea: playback.onClickTimeArea,
    onActionResizeStart: clipResize.onActionResizeStart,
    onActionResizeEnd: clipResize.onActionResizeEnd,
    onChange: clipEditing.onChange,
    onOverlayChange: clipEditing.onOverlayChange,
    onTimelineDragOver: externalDrop.onTimelineDragOver,
    onTimelineDragLeave: externalDrop.onTimelineDragLeave,
    onTimelineDrop: externalDrop.onTimelineDrop,
    handleAssetDrop: assetManagement.handleAssetDrop,
    handleDeleteClip: clipEditing.handleDeleteClip,
    handleSelectedClipChange: clipEditing.handleSelectedClipChange,
    handleResetClipPosition: clipEditing.handleResetClipPosition,
    handleSplitSelectedClip: clipEditing.handleSplitSelectedClip,
    handleSplitClipAtTime: clipEditing.handleSplitClipAtTime,
    handleToggleMute: clipEditing.handleToggleMute,
    handleTrackPopoverChange: trackManagement.handleTrackPopoverChange,
    handleReorderTrack: trackManagement.handleReorderTrack,
    handleRemoveTrack: trackManagement.handleRemoveTrack,
    moveSelectedClipToTrack: trackManagement.moveSelectedClipToTrack,
    moveClipToRow: trackManagement.moveClipToRow,
    createTrackAndMoveClip: trackManagement.createTrackAndMoveClip,
    uploadFiles: dataHook.uploadFiles,
  }), [
    dataHook.compositionSize,
    dataHook.data,
    dataHook.dataRef,
    dataHook.isLoading,
    dataHook.preferences,
    dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    dataHook.scale,
    dataHook.scaleWidth,
    dataHook.selectedClip,
    dataHook.selectedClipHasPredecessor,
    dataHook.selectedClipId,
    dataHook.selectedTrack,
    dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    dataHook.setActiveClipTab,
    dataHook.setAssetPanelState,
    dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    dataHook.trackScaleMap,
    dataHook.uploadFiles,
    assetManagement.handleAssetDrop,
    assetManagement.registerGenerationAsset,
    clipEditing.handleDeleteClip,
    clipEditing.handleResetClipPosition,
    clipEditing.handleSelectedClipChange,
    clipEditing.handleSplitSelectedClip,
    clipEditing.handleSplitClipAtTime,
    clipEditing.handleToggleMute,
    clipEditing.onChange,
    clipEditing.onOverlayChange,
    clipResize.onActionResizeEnd,
    clipResize.onActionResizeStart,
    dragCoordinator.coordinator,
    dragCoordinator.editAreaRef,
    dragCoordinator.indicatorRef,
    externalDrop.onTimelineDragLeave,
    externalDrop.onTimelineDragOver,
    externalDrop.onTimelineDrop,
    playback.onClickTimeArea,
    playback.onCursorDrag,
    playback.timelineRef,
    playback.timelineWrapperRef,
    trackManagement.createTrackAndMoveClip,
    trackManagement.handleRemoveTrack,
    trackManagement.handleReorderTrack,
    trackManagement.handleTrackPopoverChange,
    trackManagement.moveClipToRow,
    trackManagement.moveSelectedClipToTrack,
  ]);

  const chrome = useMemo<TimelineChromeContextValue>(() => ({
    timelineName: runtime.timelineName ?? null,
    saveStatus: dataHook.saveStatus,
    renderStatus: dataHook.renderStatus,
    renderLog: dataHook.renderLog,
    renderDirty: dataHook.renderDirty,
    renderProgress: dataHook.renderProgress,
    renderResultUrl: dataHook.renderResultUrl,
    renderResultFilename: dataHook.renderResultFilename,
    setScaleWidth: dataHook.setScaleWidth,
    handleAddTrack: trackManagement.handleAddTrack,
    handleClearUnusedTracks: trackManagement.handleClearUnusedTracks,
    unusedTrackCount: trackManagement.unusedTrackCount,
    handleAddText: clipEditing.handleAddText,
    reloadFromServer: dataHook.reloadFromServer,
    startRender: dataHook.startRender,
  }), [
    runtime.timelineName,
    dataHook.reloadFromServer,
    dataHook.renderDirty,
    dataHook.renderLog,
    dataHook.renderProgress,
    dataHook.renderResultFilename,
    dataHook.renderResultUrl,
    dataHook.renderStatus,
    dataHook.saveStatus,
    dataHook.setScaleWidth,
    dataHook.startRender,
    clipEditing.handleAddText,
    trackManagement.handleAddTrack,
    trackManagement.handleClearUnusedTracks,
    trackManagement.unusedTrackCount,
  ]);

  const playbackValue = useMemo<TimelinePlaybackContextValue>(() => ({
    currentTime: playback.currentTime,
    previewRef: playback.previewRef,
    playerContainerRef: playback.playerContainerRef,
    onPreviewTimeUpdate: playback.onPreviewTimeUpdate,
    formatTime: playback.formatTime,
  }), [
    playback.currentTime,
    playback.formatTime,
    playback.onPreviewTimeUpdate,
    playback.playerContainerRef,
    playback.previewRef,
  ]);

  return {
    editor,
    chrome,
    playback: playbackValue,
  };
}

item.text: The remaining dead path is exactly what the plan warned about: `onChange` only existed to consume library-managed resize output. Since `TimelineCanvas` now drives resize directly into `onActionResizeEnd`, I’m removing that context wiring rather than leaving a stale callback behind.

The editor swap compiles. I’m doing a quick verification pass now on three things the batch explicitly called out: that `TimelineEditor` no longer references the library component/CSS, that `onChange` is gone from the editor path, and that the label pane can drive `setScrollTop` back into the canvas for two-way alignment.

dist/assets/blogPosts-C3ppBhP-.js.map:1:{"version":3,"file":"blogPosts-C3ppBhP-.js","sources":["../../docs/blog/ios-safari-fixed-overlays.md?raw","../../src/pages/Blog/blogPosts.ts"],"sourcesContent":["export default \"---\\ntitle: Fixing Fixed Overlays on iPad Safari (And Why Your Component Library Matters)\\nslug: ios-safari-fixed-overlays\\ndate: 2025-06-12\\ndescription: Three CSS fixes for iOS Safari's viewport-shifting behavior on fixed overlays, and why component library choice matters for touch-heavy apps.\\nauthor: Peter O'Malley\\n---\\n\\n# Fixing Fixed Overlays on iPad Safari (And Why Your Component Library Matters)\\n\\nWe built a fullscreen lightbox — image on the left, scrollable panel on the right. It worked everywhere except iPad Safari, where touching the image area shifted the entire overlay upward, leaving a gap at the bottom. Closing and reopening made it worse.\\n\\n![Before: overlay shifts upward on touch, leaving a gap at the bottom](/images/blog/ios-safari-before.png)\\n\\nIf you've built a modal or lightbox for iPad Safari, you've probably hit some version of this. There are a lot of partial answers online. The reason none of them fully worked for us is that there are multiple interacting issues.\\n\\n## The Three Fixes\\n\\n### 1. `touch-action: none` on non-scrollable regions (the main fix)\\n\\nTouching and dragging on a non-scrollable part of the overlay (like an image display area) causes iOS Safari to rubber-band the visual viewport. The overlay shifts, a gap appears, and it slowly bounces back.\\n\\nThe fix is `touch-action: none` on those regions. But there's a trap that took us the longest to find:\\n\\n### The `touch-action` Intersection Rule\\n\\n**`touch-action` uses ancestor intersection, not child override.**\\n\\nMost CSS properties let children override parents. `touch-action` doesn't. The browser computes the effective touch action by intersecting the values of the target element and ALL its ancestors. `none ∩ pan-y = none`.\\n\\nThis means if you put `touch-action: none` on a parent container to block scrolling, and then try to re-enable it on a scrollable child panel with `touch-action: pan-y` — **it doesn't work**. The child's `pan-y` is silently ignored.\\n\\n```\\n❌ DON'T — kills scrolling everywhere\\n\\n<div style=\\\"touch-action: none\\\">          ← ancestor\\n  <div style=\\\"touch-action: pan-y\\\">      ← ignored! intersection = none\\n    scrollable panel\\n  </div>\\n</div>\\n```\\n\\nThe fix is to use **sibling isolation**: put `touch-action: none` on siblings of your scroll containers, not on shared ancestors.\\n\\n```\\n✅ DO — sibling isolation\\n\\n<div>                                         ← neutral ancestor\\n  <div style=\\\"touch-action: none\\\">           ← non-scrollable sibling\\n    image display, media, etc.\\n  </div>\\n  <div style=\\\"overscroll-behavior: contain\\\"> ← scrollable sibling\\n    scrollable panel\\n  </div>\\n</div>\\n```\\n\\nThis way the scroll container's ancestor chain is clean, and its `touch-action` resolves as expected. The non-scrollable sibling blocks browser gestures only within its own subtree.\\n\\n### 2. `overscroll-behavior: contain` on scrollable regions\\n\\nWhen a user scrolls your side panel and hits the boundary, the scroll event \\\"chains\\\" to the next scrollable ancestor — typically the body. This can trigger iOS Safari's address bar animation, which shifts the viewport.\\n\\n**Fix:** `overscroll-behavior: contain` on every scrollable container inside the overlay:\\n\\n```css\\n.scrollable-panel {\\n  overflow-y: auto;\\n  overscroll-behavior: contain; /* Safari 16+ */\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### 3. Don't use `100dvh` on fixed overlays\\n\\nOn a `position: fixed` overlay, you might set `height: 100dvh` to fill the viewport. On iOS Safari, `dvh` changes as the address bar shows and hides, so your overlay resizes in real time.\\n\\nUse `inset: 0` instead, which fills the viewport stably:\\n\\n```css\\n.overlay {\\n  position: fixed;\\n  inset: 0; /* stable — doesn't change with address bar */\\n  /* NOT height: 100dvh */\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Defense in depth: `position: fixed` on the body\\n\\nIf your `touch-action` coverage isn't complete across all layout variants (e.g., you have mobile layouts, portrait layouts, or edge cases where touches can land outside your non-scrollable regions), you may also want to lock the body with `position: fixed` + scroll position save/restore:\\n\\n```js\\n// On open\\nconst scrollY = window.scrollY;\\ndocument.body.style.position = 'fixed';\\ndocument.body.style.top = `-${scrollY}px`;\\ndocument.body.style.width = '100%';\\ndocument.body.style.overflow = 'hidden';\\n\\n// On close\\ndocument.body.style.position = '';\\ndocument.body.style.top = '';\\ndocument.body.style.width = '';\\ndocument.body.style.overflow = '';\\nwindow.scrollTo(0, scrollY);\\n```\\n\\nNote: `overflow: hidden` alone doesn't work on iOS Safari ([WebKit bug #153852](https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=153852)) — `position: fixed` is what actually prevents touch-scrolling the body. The scroll save/restore prevents dirty state from accumulating across open/close cycles.\\n\\nThis is a heavier approach and can conflict with other code that manipulates body styles, so treat it as a safety net rather than a primary fix.\\n\\n## Summary\\n\\n| iOS Safari behavior | Fix |\\n|---|---|\\n| Touches rubber-band visual viewport | `touch-action: none` on non-scrollable **siblings** |\\n| Scroll chains to body at boundaries | `overscroll-behavior: contain` on scroll containers |\\n| `100dvh` resizes with address bar | Use `inset: 0` instead on fixed elements |\\n\\n## Why Your Component Library Matters\\n\\nBefore finding this solution, we spent a day trying to fix the same issue using Radix UI's Dialog. Radix ships with RemoveScroll, its own scroll-locking system that intercepts touch events and manipulates body styles. On iOS Safari, it doesn't fully work — [radix-ui/primitives#3078](https://github.com/radix-ui/primitives/issues/3078) and [radix-ui/website#424](https://github.com/radix-ui/website/issues/424) have been open for a while.\\n\\nThe harder problem was that we couldn't easily work around it. Applying our own `position: fixed` body fix conflicted with RemoveScroll's body manipulation. Adding `touch-action: none` interacted unpredictably with Radix's event interception. We were debugging two systems at once — iOS Safari and the framework's scroll lock — and couldn't tell which was causing what.\\n\\nWe switched to [Base UI](https://base-ui.com)'s Dialog, which let us opt out of all the implicit behavior:\\n\\n```jsx\\n<Dialog.Root\\n  open={true}\\n  modal={false}              // No scroll locking — we handle it\\n  disablePointerDismissal    // No event interception — we handle it\\n  onOpenChange={(open, e) => {\\n    e.cancel();              // No internal state changes\\n    e.allowPropagation();    // No event suppression\\n  }}\\n>\\n```\\n\\nWith `modal={false}`, Base UI does no scroll locking, no touch event interception, and no body style manipulation. It gives you a portal, a backdrop, a popup, and accessibility attributes. The rest is yours.\\n\\nThe tradeoff is real — Base UI made us do the work ourselves. But the fixes in this post worked because nothing in the library was silently undoing them.\\n\\n## Takeaway\\n\\nFor touch-heavy applications on iOS Safari, pay attention to what your component library does implicitly. Built-in scroll locking is convenient until it breaks on your target platform and you can't opt out. Sometimes you want a library that gives you primitives and gets out of the way.\\n\\n![After: overlay stays locked in place, no shifting](/images/blog/ios-safari-after.png)\\n\\nYou can see our full implementation in [this commit](https://github.com/banodoco/Reigh/commit/bc40f9d5).\\n\"","// Load all .md files from docs/blog/ at build time\nconst modules = import.meta.glob('/docs/blog/*.md', {\n  eager: true,\n  query: '?raw',\n  import: 'default',\n}) as Record<string, string>;\n\ninterface BlogPost {\n  title: string;\n  slug: string;\n  date: string;\n  description: string;\n  author: string;\n  content: string;\n}\n\nfunction parseFrontmatter(raw: string): { meta: Record<string, string>; content: string } {\n  const match = raw.match(/^---\\r?\\n([\\s\\S]*?)\\r?\\n---\\r?\\n([\\s\\S]*)$/);\n  if (!match) return { meta: {}, content: raw };\n\n  const meta: Record<string, string> = {};\n  for (const line of match[1].split('\\n')) {\n    const idx = line.indexOf(':');\n    if (idx === -1) continue;\n    const key = line.slice(0, idx).trim();\n    const value = line.slice(idx + 1).trim();\n    meta[key] = value;\n  }\n  return { meta, content: match[2] };\n}\n\nexport const blogPosts: BlogPost[] = Object.values(modules)\n  .map((raw) => {\n    const { meta, content } = parseFrontmatter(raw);\n    if (!meta.title || !meta.slug) return null;\n    return {\n      title: meta.title,\n      slug: meta.slug,\n      date: meta.date ?? '',\n      description: meta.description ?? '',\n      author: meta.author ?? '',\n      content,\n    };\n  })\n  .filter((p): p is BlogPost => p !== null)\n  .sort((a, b) => b.date.localeCompare(a.date));\n\nexport function getPostBySlug(slug: string): BlogPost | undefined {\n  return blogPosts.find((p) => p.slug === slug);\n}\n"],"names":["__vite_glob_0_0","modules","parseFrontmatter","raw","match","meta","line","idx","key","value","blogPosts","content","p","a","b","getPostBySlug","slug"],"mappings":"AAAA,MAAAA,EAAe;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA;AAAA,ECCTC,EAAU,OAAA,OAAA,CAAA,0CAAAD,CAAA,CAAA,EAehB,SAASE,EAAiBC,EAAgE,CACxF,MAAMC,EAAQD,EAAI,MAAM,4CAA4C,EACpE,GAAI,CAACC,EAAO,MAAO,CAAE,KAAM,CAAA,EAAI,QAASD,CAAA,EAExC,MAAME,EAA+B,CAAA,EACrC,UAAWC,KAAQF,EAAM,CAAC,EAAE,MAAM;AAAA,CAAI,EAAG,CACvC,MAAMG,EAAMD,EAAK,QAAQ,GAAG,EAC5B,GAAIC,IAAQ,GAAI,SAChB,MAAMC,EAAMF,EAAK,MAAM,EAAGC,CAAG,EAAE,KAAA,EACzBE,EAAQH,EAAK,MAAMC,EAAM,CAAC,EAAE,KAAA,EAClCF,EAAKG,CAAG,EAAIC,CACd,CACA,MAAO,CAAE,KAAAJ,EAAM,QAASD,EAAM,CAAC,CAAA,CACjC,CAEO,MAAMM,EAAwB,OAAO,OAAOT,CAAO,EACvD,IAAKE,GAAQ,CACZ,KAAM,CAAE,KAAAE,EAAM,QAAAM,GAAYT,EAAiBC,CAAG,EAC9C,MAAI,CAACE,EAAK,OAAS,CAACA,EAAK,KAAa,KAC/B,CACL,MAAOA,EAAK,MACZ,KAAMA,EAAK,KACX,KAAMA,EAAK,MAAQ,GACnB,YAAaA,EAAK,aAAe,GACjC,OAAQA,EAAK,QAAU,GACvB,QAAAM,CAAA,CAEJ,CAAC,EACA,OAAQC,GAAqBA,IAAM,IAAI,EACvC,KAAK,CAACC,EAAGC,IAAMA,EAAE,KAAK,cAAcD,EAAE,IAAI,CAAC,EAEvC,SAASE,EAAcC,EAAoC,CAChE,OAAON,EAAU,KAAME,GAAMA,EAAE,OAASI,CAAI,CAC9C"}

return;\n        }\n\n        // Check if user has completed onboarding\n        const { data: userData, error } = await supabase().from('users')\n          .select('onboarding_completed')\n };\n\n    checkOnboardingStatus();\n\n    return () => {\n      if (timeoutId) clearTimeout(timeoutId);\n    };\n  }, []);\n\n  const completeOnboarding = async () => {\n    try {\n      const { data: {\n      import('@/shared/components/ProductTour').catch((error) => {\n        normalizeAndPresentError(error, {\n          context: 'useOnboardingFlow.preloadProductTour',\n          showToast: thumbnailUrl?: string | null;\n  styleReferenceStrength: number;\n  subjectStrength: number;\n  subjectDescription: string;\n  inThisScene: boolean;\n  referenceMode?: 'style' | 'subject' | recropAllReferences(\n  references: RecropReferenceInput[],\n  newAspectRatio: string,\n  onProgress?: (current: number, total: number) => void\n): Promise<RecropReferenceInput[]> {\n  \n  const (!ref.styleReferenceImageOriginal) {\n      reprocessed.push(ref);\n      onProgress?.(i + 1, references.length);\n      continue;\n    }\n    \n    try {\n      // Fetch the original image\n      const response = await fetch(ref.styleReferenceImageOriginal);\n      if (!response.ok) {\n        throw new Error(`Failed to fetch image: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);\n      }\n      const blob = await response.blob();\n      \n      // Convert to data URL for processing\n      const dataURL = await new Promise<string>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const reader = new reader.readAsDataURL(blob);\n      });\n      \n      // Reprocess for new aspect ratio\n      const processedDataURL = await processStyleReferenceForAspectRatioString(\n        dataURL,\n        normalizeAndPresentError(processedFileResult.error, {\n          context: 'RecropReferences.dataURLtoFile',\n          showToast: false,\n          logData: = '';\n      let newThumbnailUrl = '';\n      \n      try {\n        // Generate thumbnail for reprocessed image\n        const thumbnailResult = await generateClientThumbnail(processedFile, 300, 0.8);\n\n        // Upload both main image and thumbnail\n        const uploadResult = await uploadImageWithThumbnail(processedFile, thumbnailResult.thumbnailBlob);\n        newProcessedUrl = uploadResult.imageUrl;\n        newThumbnailUrl = uploadResult.thumbnailUrl;\n        \n      } catch {\n        // Fallback to original upload flow without thumbnail\n        newProcessedUrl = await uploadImageToStorage(processedFile);\n        newThumbnailUrl = newProcessedUrl; // Use main image as fallback\n      }\n      \n      // Update reference with new processed URL and thumbnail (keep original)\n      const updatedRef = {\n        ...ref,\n        styleReferenceImage: newProcessedUrl,\n        thumbnailUrl: newThumbnailUrl,\n        updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()\n      };\n      reprocessed.push(updatedRef);\n      \n      // Report progress\n      onProgress?.(i + 1, references.length);\n      \n    } catch (error) {\n      normalizeAndPresentError(error, { context: 'RecropReferences', showToast: false });\n      // Keep the old reference unchanged on error\n      reprocessed.push(ref);\n      // Still report progress\n      onProgress?.(i + 1, references.length);\n    }\n  }\n  \n  return reprocessed;\n}\n","import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { Button } from \"@/shared/components/ui/button\";\nimport { Input } from toast.info(\"No changes detected.\");\n      onOpenChange(false);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!updates.name && !updates.aspectRatio) { // Should be caught by hasChanges, but as a safeguard\n value={projectName}\n            onChange={(e) => setProjectName(e.target.value)}\n            className=\"w-full\"\n            disabled={isUpdatingProject}\n            maxLength={30}\n          />\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"space-y-1.5\">\n          <Label htmlFor=\"aspect-ratio-settings\">\n            Aspect Ratio:\n          </Label>\n          <AspectRatioSelector\n </div>\n    </ModalContainer>\n  );\n}; \n","import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle } from
